text.title: langchain-agents-architectures Failure Modes
text.summary: Five failure modes covering tool hallucination, plan staleness, LATS cost, context bloat, and schema rigidity.

section.title: Antipatterns

antipattern.description: ReAct model calls tools not in the schema OR with invalid argument types.
antipattern.reason: `tool_choice="auto"` does not guarantee valid calls. Without `handle_tool_error=True` and Pydantic-validated inputs, the run aborts on the first invalid call.

section.p: Symptom: run fails with KeyError on tool name or TypeError on args. Root cause: missing validation. Fix: add Pydantic `args_schema` to every tool; set `handle_tool_error=True`; return a meaningful error string so the agent can retry.

antipattern.description: Plan-and-Execute static plan does not account for intermediate results; failed step 2 leaves steps 3-N invalid.
antipattern.reason: Plan is static but execution is dynamic. Without a re-plan gate, the run continues on a broken foundation.

section.p: Symptom: downstream steps fail with errors that reference the failed step's expected output. Root cause: no re-plan gate. Fix: add a conditional edge after each executor node that checks whether the remaining plan is still valid; trigger re-planning if not.

antipattern.description: LATS configured with 3 candidates × 5 evaluation rounds = 15+ LLM calls per problem.
antipattern.reason: Cost scales poorly with search depth. Deeper trees explode in cost; in production this becomes the dominant agent expense.

section.p: Symptom: LATS branches' LLM bill exceeds the rest of the agent stack. Root cause: deep search by default. Fix: limit to 2 branches × 3 depth = 6 paths; score with Haiku, expand with Sonnet (~70% cost reduction).

antipattern.description: Long ReAct runs accumulate verbose tool outputs in state until the context window fills.
antipattern.reason: LangChain does not auto-truncate; a 20-step run with verbose outputs can consume 50K+ tokens.

section.p: Symptom: agent stops mid-run with context-window error. Root cause: no truncation. Fix: truncate tool outputs to ~2K characters before appending to state; offload anything larger per `filesystem-as-working-memory`.

antipattern.description: Changing `PlanExecuteState` mid-project requires migrating every checkpointed state.
antipattern.reason: LangGraph TypedDict schemas serialize into the checkpointer; schema changes break in-flight runs.

section.p: Symptom: deploy of new schema breaks all running agents. Root cause: backward-incompatible schema change. Fix: version state schemas (`v1`, `v2`); migrator function reads old, writes new; deprecate old version after grace period.
